title: W. Jonas Reger
text:
Email
  LinkedIn
  Github

      
My name is Jonas! I am a Graduate Student studying Data Science within the Statistics Department at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign (UIUC). I taught Statistics and Data Science in various roles as an Instructor and Teaching Assistant in university courses as well as research and education programs for equity, diversity, and inclusion for students of diverse backgrounds.
I am a self-taught programmer of 10+ years with great interests of applying computational skills to diverse fields related to Data Science. While my general interests extends broadly across Data Science, my core interests are Natural Language Processing and Machine Learning.

title: P-Value Calculator
Resume
text:
Projects & Publications
Unsupervised Learning & Clustering Algorithms

Unsupervised Learning: Popular Streaming Platforms

Processed a mixed variable dataset using Pandas for clustering analysis and performed exploratory data analysis to find correlations for features such as Genre, Age, Country, IMDb Score, etc.
Leveraged Agglomerative Hierarchical Clustering and K-Modes Clustering to identify targeted consumer groups across streaming platforms like Amazon Prime Video and Netflix based on available films and viewer ratings
Visualized clusterability and accuracy of target group clusters formed by each algorithm using evaluation metrics such as cophenetic correlation coefficient, t-SNE plots and average silhouette scores/plots




Experience
Sandia National Laboratories — Albuquerque, NM

R&D Graduate Virtual Intern — MARTIANS Program

Investigated social media data to ascertain evidence of social influence strategy
Leveraged Python and PushShift API wrappers to aggregate data from social media sites
Utilized Huggingface Natural Language Processing models for sentiment, topic, intent, and utterance analysis
Developed Random Forest and Deep Neural Network models for predicting user engagement


Discovery Partners Institute, University of Illinois at Chicago — Chicago, IL

Data Science Lead Virtual Instructor — Digital Scholars Program

Designed and instructed “Discovering Data Science” course for a high school summer STEM program
Taught data science topics in programming with Python, statistics, ethics, and real-world applications
Guided students through data analysis projects using Chicago data, to discover something about their community


University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign — Urbana, IL

Teaching Assistant — Statistics Department

Facilitated student learning objectives in class and office hours covering data science topics in programming with Python/R, data management, probability, and statistics
Wrote and audited course materials for course development and student assessment using Python/R and GitHub
Supported diverse students in data science research projects for the Blackwell Summer Scholars Program
Designed and maintained the STAT 100 course website using Jekyll and Github Pages, which serves 1000+ students each semester, providing useful course information and education materials [https://stat100website.web.illinois.edu]




Education

University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign

Master of Science in Statistics — 3.93/4.00 — May 2023
Bachelor of Science in Statistics, Minor in Mathematics — 3.56/4.00 — May 2021 — Highest Distinction

Parkland College

Associate of Science in Computer Science — 3.32/4.00 — May 2018

title: Cancer Research Publication for American Cancer Society
text:
In summer 2013, I participated in a research project with American Cancer Society and University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign. The objective of the experiment was to analyze cellular reactions to simple adhesion peptides with combinations of cancer implicated growth factors. Peptides that contained growth factors that enhance cancer cellular signaling showed greater greater potential in acetylated lysine expression than those that include cancer cellular signaling inhibitors
Link
Using peptide surfaces to study the reprogramming of malignant melanoma cells towards identification of a cancer stem cell state
Abstract
The microenvironment that surrounds cells contains many different components of molecules such as proteins and growth factors that influence cellular behavior. By creating microenvironments to influence cells, it will allow us to use materials to control cellular behaviors for biomedical applications in cancer diagnosis and treatment. In this project, we used solid phase peptide synthesis to create short peptides derived from adhesion and signaling proteins. We employed a method using copper-catalyzed azide-alkyne cycloaddition on surfaces to arrange the peptides in self-assembled monolayers (SAM) to see how mouse melanoma cells would react to different combinations of peptides and growth factors in terms of marker expressions. Adhesion peptides and combinations of cancer implicated growth factors (ATWLPPR, HTSDQTN, CPQPRPL, and SVVYGLR) were immobilized onto gold-coated coverslips. B16F10 melanoma cells were later seeded and stained for markers of the cellular epigenetic state and pluripotency, acetylated lysine and Nanog respectively. Peptides involved in epidermal growth factor signaling increased lysine acetylation in adherent B16F10s compared to the adhesion peptide alone. This platform will prove useful for the discovery of synthetic surfaces that control melanoma epigenetics and regulate gene expression for studying oncogenesis and reprogramming melanoma to a cancer stem cell state.

title: STAT 100 Course Website
text:
Designed the STAT 100 course website initially in Fall 2019 and launched it publicly as the official course website under Karle Flanagan’s GitHub repository. I implemented numerous updates and modifications to it as the main web developer until the end of summer in 2022. Currently, I am an advisor for any developer matters, but no longer maintaining the content updates. The goal of designing the website was to make the site more easily accessible and more aesthetically pleasing to students. The site serves all STAT 100 students, which makes up approximately 3000+ students a year at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign.
Link
STAT 100 Official Course Website
STAT 100 Website GitHub Repository

title: Walmart Sales During Stormy Weather Data Analysis
text:
In fall 2019, I contributed to a class group project for STAT 425 (Appled Regression & Design) at University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign. The objective of the project was to predict the number of items sold at Walmart stores during stormy weather (within 3 days of a storm). The data was from an old Kaggle competition, from which we drew our emphasis on predictive power over interpretability of our models.
Link
Walmart Sales and Weather Data Analysis

title: Reducing Carbon Monoxide Emissions of Gas Turbines Analysis
text:
In spring 2021, I contributed to a class group project for STAT 443 (Statistical Consulting) at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign. The objective of the project was to analyze gas turbine data and find interpretable features towards reducing carbon monoxide emissions. The goal of the project was to communicate our results to a mock client as statistical consultants.
Link
Documents on GitHub

title: Discovering Data Science Course Website
text:
In summer 2021, I developed and taught an intro to data science course for Chicago high school students at Discovery Partners Institute. As lead instructor, I developed a new curriculum to pilot the Data Science track course of the Digital Scholars Program. The goal was to expose students with diverse cultural and academic backgrounds to data science topics in a learning-friendly environment. Many students did their own data analysis project on Chicago-related data at the conclusion of the program.
Link
Discovering Data Science Course Website
Discovering Data Science Website GitHub Repository

title: Movies on Streaming Platforms Clustering Analysis
text:
In spring 2021, I contributed to a group project for STAT 430 (Unsupervised Learning) at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign. The objective of the project was explore a movies streaming platform dataset from Kaggle and detect any underlying clustering structures using methods and tools in python.
Link
Documents on GitHub

title: Reducing Carbon Monoxide Emissions of Gas Turbines Analysis
text:
In spring 2021, I contributed to a class group project for STAT 443 (Statistical Consulting) at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign. The objective of the project was to analyze gas turbine data from the UCI repository and find interpretable features towards reducing carbon monoxide emissions. The goal of the project was to communicate our results to a mock client as statistical consultants.
Link
Documents on GitHub
